message:
feat(demo_showcase): implement runtime Config struct with validation

- Add config module with complete Config struct
- Add ColorMode enum (Auto, Always, Never)
- Add AnimationMode enum (Enabled, Reduced, Disabled)
- Add ConfigError for validation failures
- Implement Config::from_cli() for CLI to config mapping
- Add config validation for theme_file and files_root paths
- Add to_diagnostic_string() for diagnostics output
- Add serde Serialize/Deserialize for Config and ThemePreset
- Wire Config into main() for proper initialization
- 17 comprehensive unit tests for config handling

The Config struct is now the single source of truth for runtime
toggles, testable without CLI parsing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2omz","title":"demo_showcase page: Wizard/Forms (huh integration)","description":"An embedded workflow that looks like a real onboarding/deploy wizard.

Must showcase:
- huh fields (input/select/confirm/textarea)
- validation + errors
- multi-step flow integrated into the app shell

Acceptance:
- Completing the wizard triggers state changes visible elsewhere (e.g., starts a deployment/job).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:20.248709115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:20.268073161Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","forms","huh","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2omz","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:20.268047484Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2pzh","title":"demo_showcase: pages + workflows","description":"Implement the major product surfaces of `demo_showcase` as *real application pages* (not a component gallery), and wire them together with realistic workflows.

Why:
- The showcase only “proves the stack” if the user can do meaningful, multi-step things: inspect a system, change settings, trigger work, and observe outcomes.
- Pages force integration: bubbletea routing, bubbles components, lipgloss layout/theming, glamour rendering, harmonica motion, charmed_log observability.

Design goals:
- Cohesive product story: this should feel like a premium ops console / developer tool.
- Each page is internally coherent (a workflow), and also participates in cross-page flows (e.g., a wizard creates jobs; jobs link to logs; docs explain the current screen).
- Strong information architecture: fast navigation, clear “where am I”, consistent commands.

Target pages (can evolve, but must stay comprehensive):
- Dashboard: “wow” overview, live metrics, animated deltas, drill-down.
- Jobs/Tasks: realistic queue with progress, filtering, actions, details pane.
- Logs: live tail + filtering/search + export/copy.
- Docs: in-app knowledge base rendered by glamour + search.
- Wizard/Forms: a guided flow (huh) that triggers simulated actions.
- Files: filepicker + preview (text/markdown) and common UX (errors, permissions).
- Settings: runtime toggles + theme switching + diagnostics.

Cross-page workflows (minimum):
- “Create/Deploy” (Wizard) -> creates/updates Jobs -> Jobs details links to Logs timeline.
- Docs contains a “what is this screen?” entry per page, and deep links back to pages.
- Settings changes (theme/animations/mouse/no-color/etc.) take effect immediately and are reflected across all pages.

Implementation constraints:
- Keep a single app shell (header/sidebar/main/footer) and swap page bodies via routing.
- All pages must respect:
  - Config toggles (`--no-alt-screen`, `--no-color`, `--no-animations`, etc.).
  - Resize responsiveness (reflow layout) and consistent keybindings.
  - Deterministic demo data mode for tests/E2E.

Acceptance:
- A first-time user can explore all pages without reading code and immediately “gets it”.
- Navigation feels consistent (back, page switch, help overlay) and never strands the user.
- Every page uses multiple crates in an integrated way.

Testing:
- Unit tests per page for:
  - update logic (message handling, filtering, selection stability)
  - view invariants (no panics, stable layout given fixed terminal size)
- E2E scenarios cover:
  - smoke tour across every page
  - wizard -> jobs -> logs correlation
  - docs render + search + resize reflow
  - file picker navigation + preview
  - settings toggles + theme switching
  - logs copy/export actions

Notes:
- Avoid “toy dashboards” with random numbers; prefer a deterministic simulation that looks plausible.
- Favor usability over maximal density: always keep a clear primary path + shortcuts for power users.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:08.023630690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:08.434450914Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","pages"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pzh","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:08.039286770Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2q4a","title":"Settings: theme picker + preview","description":"Implement premium theme selection UX inside Settings.

Why:
- Theme switching is one of the strongest "wow" factors in the Charm ecosystem.
- It’s also a practical user feature: dark/light terminals, low-color terminals, screenshots, etc.

Requirements:
- Show available theme presets with *meaningful previews* (not just names):
  - Include sample UI primitives: badges/chips, buttons, table header, log level tags, code block style.
  - Make it obvious how the theme affects the whole app.
- Keyboard-friendly:
  - up/down to select, enter to apply
  - optional filter/search for theme names
- Apply immediately and globally:
  - header/status should reflect current theme name
  - pages re-render using the new theme without requiring navigation

Integration notes:
- The theme picker is a UI on top of the underlying theme switching mechanism (`demo_showcase theme: live theme switching UI`).
- Ensure theme selection remains coherent with no-color/ASCII mode (theme changes should not re-enable color).

Acceptance:
- Theme switching is discoverable, instant, and never leaves mismatched colors or broken layouts.
- Switching themes produces a toast/log entry so users feel it "took".","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:59.853422990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:44.767991832Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","settings","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q4a","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:44.767949273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q4a","depends_on_id":"bd-274o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:59.881338818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q4a","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:49.570476996Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q4a","depends_on_id":"bd-k52c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:52.272593124Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2q95","title":"demo_showcase: runtime Config struct + clap/env mapping","description":"Implement the *actual* runtime configuration layer for demo_showcase.

Rationale:
- Many features need toggles (theme, no-color, no-animations, mouse, seed, alt-screen, syntax).
- If every feature reads clap/env directly, the code becomes untestable and inconsistent.

Deliverables:
- `Config` struct representing all runtime options with explicit defaults.
- CLI parsing (clap) that maps args -> Config.
- Environment variable integration + precedence rules (e.g., `NO_COLOR` overrides unless `--force-color`, etc.).
- Validation (e.g., widths > 0, seed parse, theme name exists).
- Export the resolved config in About/Diagnostics.

Must cover options mentioned across the plan:
- `--theme <name|auto>` + optional `--theme-file <path>`
- `--seed <u64>`
- `--no-animations`
- `--no-mouse`
- `--no-color` (force ASCII)
- `--no-alt-screen` (so bubbletea println/printf can be demonstrated cleanly)
- `--self-check` (headless)
- Files/fixtures root (for Files page + E2E determinism): `--files-root <path>` (name TBD)
- SSH mode args (if enabled): addr, host key
- Syntax toggles (if enabled): syntax on/off, line numbers

Acceptance:
- There is a single source of truth for runtime toggles.
- Unit tests can construct Config directly without parsing CLI.
- CLI parsing is thin and well-logged.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:56.461191204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:52.990531476Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q95","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:07.927570580Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q95","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:06.656528955Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q95","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:56.477231837Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Acceptance:
- There is a single source of truth for runtime toggles.
- Unit tests can construct Config directly without parsing CLI.
- CLI parsing is thin and well-logged.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:56.461191204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:41:40.548492408Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:41:40.548426525Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q95","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:07.927570580Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q95","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:06.656528955Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q95","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:56.477231837Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2q98","title":"Add bubbletea Println/Printf unmanaged output and rich error types","description":"Implement Bubbletea Println/Printf unmanaged output and richer error types to match Go ergonomics.

Scope:
- Add Program-level Println/Printf helpers that write outside the TUI renderer.
- Ensure output respects raw/alt-screen modes and restores terminal appropriately.
- Define richer error types for program failures (I/O, renderer, terminal).
- Add unit tests and integration tests for unmanaged output.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Println/Printf APIs documented and tested in both sync/async Program modes.
- Rich error types carry context and are surfaced to callers.
- Integration tests show unmanaged output without corrupting screen.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs, crates/bubbletea/src/error.rs (if present), tests in crates/bubbletea or tests/conformance.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:33.022679833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:54:15.639129033Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:54:15.639065976Z","close_reason":"Completed: println/printf commands and rich error types are fully implemented. Commands export from crate root, handle alt-screen mode, unit tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2qq7","title":"Wizard: integrate with demo data + trigger jobs/deployments","description":"Wire wizard completion into the demo backend.

Implementation notes:
- Use the shared domain action API (`demo_showcase data: action API …`) so the Wizard and Jobs page don't depend on each other.

Examples:
- Starting a deployment creates a new Deployment + associated Job.
- Emits log lines and toasts.

Acceptance:
- Finishing the wizard immediately changes Dashboard/Jobs/Logs content.
- Behavior is covered by unit tests at the action-API layer.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:03.497458412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:56.227088421Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data","demo_showcase","forms"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:55.728413037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-28l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:50.039176856Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-2omz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:03.519336622Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:47.884659245Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:52.897573354Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qx1","title":"demo_showcase page: Logs (styled + filterable)","description":"A live log viewer page that feels like a production-grade log tailer.

Why:
- Logging is where real apps live and die; this is the most convincing place to show `charmed_log` styling + ergonomics.
- It also provides the “ground truth” for cross-page workflows: Jobs should link to logs; errors should explain themselves.

Must showcase:
- `charmed_log`-styled output:
  - level coloring (trace/debug/info/warn/error)
  - structured fields (service, job_id, request_id, duration, user)
  - readable formatting even at narrow widths
- Bubbles viewport:
  - smooth scrolling, jump to top/bottom
  - follow mode (“tail -f”) that pauses when the user scrolls up
  - optional line wrapping toggle
- Filtering/search:
  - filter by level and service
  - query search with match highlight + next/prev navigation
  - correlation filters (e.g., “show logs for selected job”)
- Actions:
  - copy selected range or last N lines
  - export to a file (with a clear artifact path) and show toast confirmation
  - clear/reset view (without breaking follow mode)

Data requirements:
- Log stream is deterministic in test mode and correlated with the Jobs simulation.
- Includes occasional warnings/errors so filtering and drill-down are meaningful.

Acceptance:
- Looks and feels like a real log tailer (usable, not flashy-for-flashy’s-sake).
- Filtering is instant and predictable; follow mode never fights the user.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filter + search correctness (stable ordering, edge cases)
  - follow mode logic (scroll up pauses, scroll bottom resumes)
  - export/copy behavior produces expected artifacts/messages
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - logs copy/export/clear scenario (verifies artifacts + JSONL logs)

Notes:
- Avoid “fake rainbow logs”; prioritize legibility and density.
- Ensure `--no-color` / ASCII modes still produce clear output.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:22.489282051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:56.936627Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","demo_showcase","logs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qx1","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:22.508975562Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
